'''Jason Paul Cayless'''<ref>[http://nzleague.co.nz/kiwis/player_profile.php?letter=C&id=699 CAYLESS, JASON PAUL 2002 – 2006 – KIWI #699] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120402171556/http://nzleague.co.nz/kiwis/player_profile.php?letter=C&id=699 |date=2 April 2012}} ''nzleague.co.nz''</ref> (born 15 January 1980) is a former [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a [[Rugby league positions#Prop forwards|prop]] in the 2000s and 2010s. He played for the [[Parramatta Eels]], [[Sydney Roosters]] and the [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[NRL]] and [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]].

He won the [[2002 Telstra Premiership]] and the [[2003 World Club Challenge]] with the [[Sydney Roosters|Roosters]], and the [[2006 Challenge Cup]], [[2006 Super League Grand Final]], [[2007 Challenge Cup]] and the [[2007 World Club Challenge]] with the [[St Helens R.F.C.|Saints]].

==Background==
Cayless was born in [[Sydney, New South Wales]], Australia. He is of [[Māori people|Maori]] descent.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=148688|title = Rugby League: Nikau delighted with Maori squad}}</ref> He is the younger brother of [[Nathan Cayless]].

==Club career==
===Parramatta Eels===
Cayless started his career in the [[National Rugby League]] with the [[Parramatta Eels]] alongside his older brother, [[Nathan Cayless]], who had already been with the club for three years.

In 2001 he made his representative début for [[New South Wales Country rugby league team|New South Wales Country Origin]].

===Sydney Roosters===
The following year Cayless moved to the [[Sydney Roosters]] and played for them at prop forward in their [[2002 NRL Grand Final]] win over the [[New Zealand Warriors]].

Having won the [[2002 NRL Premiership]], the Roosters traveled to England to play the [[2003 World Club Challenge]] against [[Super League]] champions, [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]]. Cayless played at prop forward in Sydney's victory.

He played at prop for the Sydney Roosters in the [[2003 NRL grand final]] which was lost to the [[Penrith Panthers]].

Cayless played for the Roosters at prop forward in their [[2004 NRL grand final]] loss to cross-Sydney rivals, the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Bulldogs]].

===St Helens===
Cayless played for St Helens as a prop forward in their [[2006 Challenge Cup]] Final victory against the [[Huddersfield Giants]].

St Helens reached the [[2006 Super League Grand final]] to be contested against [[Hull F.C.]] and Cayless played as a prop forward in Saints' 26–4 victory.

As 2006 Super League champions, St Helens faced 2006 NRL Premiers the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[2007 World Club Challenge]]. Cayless played at prop forward in the Saints' 18–14 victory.
[[File:Jason Cayless.JPG|thumb|300px|Cayless playing for St Helens in 2008]]

===Wests Tigers===
After spending four seasons in the [[Super League]], in October 2009 Cayless signed with the [[Wests Tigers]] on a two-year contract starting in 2010.<ref>{{cite news|publisher=Fox Sports News (Australia)|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,26210341-23214,00.html|title=Jason Cayless signs two-year deal with Wests Tigers|date=14 October 2009|access-date=14 October 2009}}</ref> He started the first three games of the season from the bench, but injuries saw him make no further appearances for the Tigers in 2010. He announced his retirement before the start of the 2011 season.

==International career==
He made his international début for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] in 2002 and went on to play in ten Test matches for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]].
 
==Highlights==
*''First Grade Debut:'' Round 21, Parramatta v [[Wests Tigers]] at [[Parramatta Stadium]], 25 June 2000
*''National Representation:'' 11 Tests for New Zealand
*''NRL Premiership Winner:'' 2002 for Sydney Roosters
*''Super League Championship Winner:'' 2006 for St Helens
*''Carnegie Challenge Cup Winner:'' 2006 for St Helens
*''World Club Challenge Winner:'' 2007 for St Helens
